Text: The main difference is the number of document requests 2 vs 3 and the time between them This suggests that in Region B or with Adjuster Lisa or CSR Paul the communication with the customer regarding additional documents might be less effective leading to incomplete submissions and thus more back and forth

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Each document request adds roughly 20 to 30 hours of waiting time likely waiting for the customer to reply and the adjuster to review The more requests the longer the case takes

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The extra iteration of document requests and longer customer response times in Region B suggest unclear communication Customers may be confused about what is needed leading to delayed and incorrect submissions

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Implement an automated dynamic document checklist at the Submit Claim stage based on initial complexity assessment Use a rule based tool to validate uploaded documents for completeness before Evaluate Claim

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Standardize the Request Additional Documents communication templates Provide targeted training for Region B adjusters like Adjuster Lisa on how to clearly explain document requirements to claimants

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Cross train Manager Ann and potentially other senior adjusters to handle High complexity approvals to distribute the workload

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Introduce automated reminders for customers when additional documents are requested Set clear service levels for customer responses and adjuster reviews
